---
title: "pySEB Documentation"
subtitle: "Executable tutorials for the Scattering Equation Builder"
---

pySEB constructs small-angle-scattering expressions for composite structures
assembled from analytic, symbolic, and numerical subunits.

## Start learning

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Python and numerical modelling

Build an analytic diblock, evaluate q-dependent scattering, construct Debye
clouds, define numerical callbacks, parse PDB coordinates, and combine a
protein model with a Gaussian polymer.

[Open the Python tutorial](tutorials/pyseb_tutorial.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### C++ modelling

Build and run maintained C++14 programs for diblocks, micelles, nested
structures, parameter sweeps, and mixed PDB–polymer models.

[Open the C++ tutorials](tutorials/cpp_tutorials.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::
:::

## Reference documentation

The [pySEB wiki](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki) contains build
guides, user manuals, public API summaries, the complete subunit catalogue,
theory, and troubleshooting information.

## Source and examples

- [Source repository](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB)
- [Python examples](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/tree/main/pyseb/examples/python)
- [C++ examples](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/tree/main/Examples)
- [Build and installation guides](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Build-Guides)
